name: Holy Spirit University of Kaslik

tuition_model.summary: USEK publishes a graduate studies fee table in USD per credit. The official note states that the cost of a credit is determined by the school/faculty offering the course, not by the student’s home affiliation, and that the prices apply in all USEK branches.

tuition_table.category: Faculty of Arts & Sciences - humanities / social sciences / education
Faculty of Arts & Sciences - Music and Performing Arts
School of Law and Political Sciences - Law / Political Sciences
Faculty of Arts & Sciences - Communication - Cinema and Television
Faculty of Arts & Sciences - Nutrition and Food Sciences
School of Architecture & Design - Architecture / Design &Digital Media
School of Architecture & Design - Design &Digital Media
School of Architecture & Design - Interior Design
School of Engineering - Agricultural & Food Engineering
Faculty of Arts & Sciences - Chemistry and Biochemistry
Faculty of Arts & Sciences - Computer science and IT
Faculty of Arts & Sciences - science programs

fee_table.notes: Non-refundable; paid at a bank specified by USEK or at any OMT branch with no additional fees.
Paid per semester (Fall, Spring, Summer Session). Included in the first payment.
Charged in Fall and Spring semesters and included in the first payment.
Paid in fresh dollars at the market rate; subject to NSSF exemption rules for affiliated students and students over 30.
First payment includes the USD 200 registration fee.

admissions.summary: Graduate applicants must hold an undergraduate degree or equivalent recognized by the Lebanese Ministry of Education and Higher Education, receive the recommendation of the unit Graduate Admission Interview Committee, meet any program-specific requirements, submit the online graduate application with the required documents, and pay the non-refundable admission file fee.
steps: Review the graduate program and any program-specific requirements.
Pay the non-refundable USD 30 admission file fee.
Complete the admission interview and any language proficiency test if required.
For doctoral study, submit the PhD Thesis Proposal path requested by the Doctoral College or the iPTP template when applicable.

required_documents: Photocopy of the national identity card or valid passport (or individual civil extract).
Baccalaureate certificate or equivalent, certified by the Lebanese Ministry of Education and Higher Education when needed.
Certified copies of university degree equivalence(s) when needed.
Official transcript(s) for all previous academic work.
Two recommendation letters (optional at the graduate level; doctoral requirements are stricter).
doctoral_required_documents: Passport copy for foreign applicants or national identity card copy for local applicants.
Lebanese Baccalaureate certificate or equivalent, certified when required.
Equivalence of bachelor’s and master’s degrees certified by the Ministry of Education and Higher Education.
Two recommendation letters from faculty who are not prospective supervisors.
Evidence of research activity such as books, publications, and conference participations.
Individual PhD Thesis Proposal (iPTP) for candidates submitting a PhD proposal.

language_requirements.summary: USEK states that graduate applicants may be required to pass language proficiency tests if needed. For some graduate programs, the guide specifies International English Tests or the USEK Language Test; some programs are multilingual and some are not applicable.
language_requirements.note: Exact language conditions vary by program and are not centralized into a single graduate-wide score table in the official materials used here.

scholarships.summary: USEK publishes a university-wide scholarships and financial-aid hub. The official page emphasizes aid, discounts, and agreement-based reductions rather than a separate graduate-only scholarship table.
categories: Financial aid applications through the Financial Aid Office
Scholarship offers for admission with scholarship are described on the university page, but they are primarily framed around admission cycles and are not published as a separate graduate-only table
scholarships.note: Graduate-specific scholarship amounts were not published in the official materials used for this pass.

financial_aid.summary: USEK states that the Financial Aid Office helps students facing financial difficulties through discounts and formal aid applications.
items: Financial aid application through the Financial Aid Office
Special cases for siblings, OLM relatives, faculty and employees, and several partner agreements

payment_plans.summary: Graduate tuition is paid in installments rather than as a single upfront payment.
schedule: Four installments per semester for Fall and Spring
First payment is USD 1000 and includes the USD 200 registration fee
Penalty applies if deadlines in the academic calendar are missed

academic_calendar.summary: The academic calendar is published as a PDF and is used for graduate application deadlines, exam dates, results, registration, payment deadlines, and NSSF timing.

academic_regulations.summary: USEK links to student regulations from the Students menu. The graduate fee page and admissions guide reference the academic regulations for refunds, payment penalties, and other administrative rules, but a standalone graduate regulations hub was not separately captured in this pass.

accreditation.name: Initial accreditation by the New England Commission of Higher Education (NECHE)

international_students.summary: International applicants are explicitly mentioned on the doctoral admissions page and in the admissions guide through passport copies, equivalence requirements, and the use of the online postgraduate application.
items: Foreign applicants may provide a passport copy instead of the national identity card copy.
Degree equivalence must be certified when required.
Doctoral applicants must submit the same core identity and academic documents plus doctoral-specific materials.

notes: Master’s tuition is fully mapped from the official graduate fee table for the 2024-2025 reference schedule.
All 21 doctoral records intentionally remain without tuition because the official fee table used here does not publish doctoral tuition amounts.
Graduate scholarship language on the official site is broader than graduate-only and was kept at university level.
